sourceMovie.title: The Green Knight
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Green Knight (2021) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Green Knight clicked for you, The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Green Knight clicked for you, King Arthur: Legend of the Sword (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Green Knight?
faq.answer: Try The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring, King Arthur: Legend of the Sword, Kingdom of Heaven—each shares DNA with The Green Knight while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Green Knight?
faq.answer: The Green Knight (2021) is best described as Adventure, Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Green Knight worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
